channel.title: NPR Topics: Religion
channel.description: NPR's stories on U.S. and world religion, spirituality, ethics, and moral issues affecting society and culture. Subscribe to NPR Religion RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These Catholic sisters want to start a new order with a difficult mission: helping survivors of clergy abuse
item.description: Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.
item.content:encoded:
Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.

(Image credit: Natosha Via for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Adora Namigadde

item.title: These Catholic sisters want to start an order for clergy abuse survivors
item.description: There are nearly 2,000 communities of religious sisters and nuns in the Catholic church. Two nuns in Kentucky want to start the first devoted to ministering to people harmed by abuse in the church.
item.content:encoded: There are nearly 2,000 communities of religious sisters and nuns in the Catholic church. Two nuns in Kentucky want to start the first devoted to ministering to people harmed by abuse in the church.
item.dc:creator: Adora Namigadde

item.title: At Mississippi funeral, civil rights leaders call for justice for Nolan Wells, 18
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.
item.content:encoded:
The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: Why Orthodox Jews are opposing the new daylight saving bill in Congress
item.description: Orthodox Jewish organizations say the bill, if passed, would force morning prayer services to start after 9 a.m. in some parts of the country, making observant Jews late for work and school.
item.content:encoded:
Orthodox Jewish organizations say the bill, if passed, would force morning prayer services to start after 9 a.m. in some parts of the country, making observant Jews late for work and school.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Yonat Shimron

item.title: A Bible passage is at the center of a debate over how the U.S. should treat immigrants
item.description: A debate over the Bible verse Matthew 25 is pitting mainline pastors, Black protestants and the pope against evangelical politicians put on the defensive over President Trump's policies.
item.content:encoded: A debate over the Bible verse Matthew 25 is pitting mainline pastors, Black protestants and the pope against evangelical politicians put on the defensive over President Trump's policies.
item.dc:creator: Jack Jenkins

item.title: As Israel divides Jewish communities, Yiddish draws a new generation
item.description: The language app Duolingo reported that roughly 296,000 people around the world are studying Yiddish as of this year. An estimated 60% of them are under 25 years old.
item.content:encoded:
The language app Duolingo reported that roughly 296,000 people around the world are studying Yiddish as of this year. An estimated 60% of them are under 25 years old.

(Image credit: Nirvani Williams)
item.dc:creator: Nirvani Williams

item.title: Greetings from a Syrian church, infused with a mix of sweet fruit and diverse faiths
item.description: Syria's Mar Musa monastery provides the tastiest mulberries, and its services draw from Eastern Orthodox Christian and Sufi customs.
item.content:encoded: Syria's Mar Musa monastery provides the tastiest mulberries, and its services draw from Eastern Orthodox Christian and Sufi customs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Pastor Doug Wilson on why he believes the U.S. should be a Christian theocracy
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: What it means to be excommunicated from the Catholic Church for starting a schism
item.description: The Society of St. Pius X has responded defiantly to Pope Leo XIV's excommunication of the neotraditionalist group. What does the excommunication mean — and how will the schism affect the church?
item.content:encoded: The Society of St. Pius X has responded defiantly to Pope Leo XIV's excommunication of the neotraditionalist group. What does the excommunication mean — and how will the schism affect the church?
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
item.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.content:encoded:
A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
